headline: Kling 3.5 adds native multi-shot audio
summary: Kling's new version makes video with matching voices and sound built in, even across cuts. You no longer need a separate lip-sync step for story videos.
detail: Kling 3.5 is the newest version of Kuaishou's video model. The big change: it now creates the voices, sound effects, and background sound together with the picture, and keeps them in sync even when the video cuts between shots. Before, you had to make the video first and then add lip-sync and sound in separate steps. That whole extra step is now gone for story-style videos. It is live on fal.ai today.

headline: Recraft V4.2 ships vector-native style locks
summary: Recraft can now keep one look locked across a whole set of images, and the files come out as clean vectors. Great for making matching design assets fast.
detail: Recraft V4.2 adds style locks: you pick a look once, and every image you make after that keeps the same style. The output is true vector files, so logos and icons stay sharp at any size. This makes it much faster to build a full set of matching brand or motion-design assets. Available now on recraft.ai for paid plans.

headline: Trellis 3 open-sources image-to-3D at production quality
summary: Trellis 3 turns one photo into a clean, ready-to-use 3D model in under a minute, and it is free to use. Handy for making props and set pieces for scenes.
detail: Trellis 3 is Microsoft's new image-to-3D model, and the weights are free to download. You give it one photo and it builds a clean, textured 3D mesh in under a minute. The quality is good enough to use directly as props and set pieces in scenes. There is already a hosted endpoint on fal.ai if you do not want to run it yourself.

headline: ElevenLabs Foley 1 generates frame-synced sound effects
summary: Upload a video and Foley 1 adds sound effects that match what happens on screen. It saves hours of finding and placing sounds by hand.
detail: Foley 1 is a new ElevenLabs model that watches your video and writes a full sound-effects track for it. Footsteps, doors, cloth, impacts — each sound lands on the exact frame where the action happens. This normally takes hours of searching sound libraries and placing clips by hand. It works on clips up to a few minutes long and runs from the ElevenLabs site or API.

headline: Runway Aleph 2 rewrites shots with camera re-blocking
summary: Aleph 2 takes a clip you already have and shows it from a new camera angle, keeping the acting and light the same. It is like reshooting a scene without a reshoot.
detail: Aleph 2 is Runway's new shot-editing model. You give it a finished clip and describe a new camera position, and it rebuilds the shot from that angle while keeping the same acting, lighting, and mood. That means you can fix a framing mistake or get extra coverage without shooting anything again. It is rolling out now to Runway's paid tiers.

headline: DeepMind paper shows video models learn real physics
summary: New research finds that big video models quietly learn how objects move and collide, just from watching video. This could mean fewer weird physics fails in AI video.
detail: DeepMind researchers tested large video models on physics tasks they were never trained for, like predicting how stacked objects fall. The models got many of them right, which suggests they learn a rough model of real-world physics just from watching video during training. The paper also shows where this breaks down — liquids and soft objects are still hard. For AI filmmakers, it hints that the next wave of video models will make fewer impossible-motion mistakes.

headline: ComfyUI launches hosted cloud workflows
summary: ComfyUI now runs in the cloud, so you can use any workflow without a big graphics card. You can also share a working setup with one link.
detail: ComfyUI's official cloud service runs any workflow graph on rented GPUs, so you no longer need an expensive graphics card at home. Every workflow gets a share link, and anyone who opens it gets the exact same working setup. Pricing is pay-per-run with a free tier for small jobs. This removes the biggest hardware barrier to node-based AI pipelines.

headline: Higgsfield raises $180M to build an AI film studio
summary: Higgsfield got new money to grow from a video tool into a full AI film studio. More budget usually means faster features and better models for users.
detail: Higgsfield announced a $180 million round to expand from a video-generation tool into a full AI film production platform. The company says the money goes to training its own models and building out Cinema Studio. For users, funding at this size usually means faster feature releases and better model quality over the next year. It also signals that investors think AI filmmaking tools are becoming a real market.

headline: Hunyuan 3.5 video weights hit Hugging Face
summary: Tencent put its new video model online for anyone to download and run. It makes sharp 1080p clips up to 12 seconds long, free.
detail: Tencent released the weights for Hunyuan 3.5, its newest video model, as a free download on Hugging Face. It makes 1080p clips up to 12 seconds long from text or a still image. You need a serious GPU to run it, but hosted versions usually appear on fal.ai and Replicate within days. It is currently the strongest open video model you can run yourself.

headline: Ideogram 4 adds full typographic layout control
summary: Ideogram 4 lets you tell it exactly which font, size, and layout to use for text in images. Posters and title cards now come out right the first time.
detail: Ideogram 4 focuses on text inside images. You can now name the font style, set sizes, and describe the layout grid in your prompt, and the model follows it. That means posters, title cards, and menus come out correct on the first try instead of needing a design pass afterward. It is live on ideogram.ai now.
